sections.heading: In these Regulations, any word or expression to which a meaning has been assigned in
sections.summary: Defines key terms including 'data message', 'form(s)', 'signature', 'submit', 'the Act', and 'writing' based on Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2002.

requirements.text: data message includes a data message as defined in section 1 of the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2002
form(s) means a form referred to in the annexures to these Regulations or any form which is substantially similar to that form
signature includes an electronic signature as defined in section 1 of the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2002
submit means submit by data message, electronic communication, registered post, electronic mail, facsimile, or personal delivery
the Act means the Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013
writing includes writing as referred to in section 12 of the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2002

sections.source_quote: In these Regulations, any word or expression to which a meaning has been assigned in the Act has the meaning so assigned and, unless the context otherwise indicates: "data message" includes a data message as defined in section 1 of the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2002; "form(s)" as referred to in these Regulations, means a form referred to in the annexures to these Regulations or any form which is substantially similar to that form; "signature" includes an electronic signature as defined in section 1 of the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2002; "submit" means submit by (a) data message; (b) electronic communication; (c) registered post; (d) electronic mail; (e) facsimile; and (f) personal delivery; "the Act" means the Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013; and "writing" includes writing as referred to in section 12 of the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2002.

sections.heading: (1) A data subject who wishes to object to the processing of personal information
sections.summary: Data subjects must submit objections to processing on Form 1 to responsible parties for lawful processing under section 11(3)(a) of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Data subjects can object to processing via Form 1; responsible parties must provide reasonable assistance free of charge.

requirements.text: A data subject who wishes to object to the processing of personal information in terms of section 11(3)(a) of the Act, must submit the objection to the responsible party on Form 1
The responsible party, or a designated person, must render such reasonable assistance as is necessary, free of charge, to enable the data subject to make an objection on Form 1

sections.source_quote: (1) A data subject who wishes to object to the processing of personal information in terms of section 11(3)(a) of the Act, must submit the objection to the responsible party on Form 1. (2) The responsible party, or a designated person, must render such reasonable assistance as is necessary, free of charge, to enable the data subject to make an objection on Form 1.

sections.heading: (1) A data subject who wishes to request a correction or deletion of personal
sections.summary: Data subjects must submit requests for correction or deletion of personal information on Form 2 to responsible parties for lawful processing under section 24(1) of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Data subjects can request correction/deletion via Form 2; responsible parties must provide reasonable assistance free of charge.

requirements.text: A data subject who wishes to request a correction or deletion of personal information or the destruction or deletion of a record of personal information in terms of section 24(1) of the Act, must submit a request to the responsible party on Form 2
The responsible party, or a designated person, must render such reasonable assistance, as is necessary free of charge, to enable a data subject to complete Form 2

sections.source_quote: (1) A data subject who wishes to request a correction or deletion of personal information or the destruction or deletion of a record of personal information in terms of section 24(1) of the Act, must submit a request to the responsible party on Form 2. (2) The responsible party, or a designated person, must render such reasonable assistance, as is necessary free of charge, to enable a data subject to complete Form 2.

sections.heading: (1) An information officer must, in addition to the responsibilities referred to in
sections.summary: Information officers must develop compliance frameworks, conduct impact assessments, maintain manuals, and ensure internal awareness sessions for the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Information officers must implement compliance frameworks, conduct impact assessments, maintain manuals, and conduct awareness sessions.

requirements.text: An information officer must, in addition to the responsibilities referred to in section 55(1) of the Act, ensure that a compliance framework is developed, implemented, monitored and maintained

An information officer must, in addition to the responsibilities referred to in section 55(1) of the Act, ensure that a personal information impact assessment is done to ensure that adequate measures and standards exist in order to comply with the conditions for the lawful processing of personal information

An information officer must, in addition to the responsibilities referred to in section 55(1) of the Act, ensure that a manual is developed, monitored, maintained and made available as prescribed in sections 14 and 51 of the Promotion of Access to Information Act, 2000

An information officer must, in addition to the responsibilities referred to in section 55(1) of the Act, ensure that internal measures are developed together with adequate systems to process requests for information or access thereto

An information officer must, in addition to the responsibilities referred to in section 55(1) of the Act, ensure that internal awareness sessions are conducted regarding the provisions of the Act, regulations made in terms of the Act, codes of conduct, or information obtained from the Regulator
The information officer shall upon request by any person, provide copies of the manual to that person upon the payment of a fee to be determined by the Regulator from time to time

sections.source_quote: An information officer must, in addition to the responsibilities referred to in section 55(1) of the Act, ensure that- (a) a compliance framework is developed, implemented, monitored and maintained (b) a personal information impact assessment is done to ensure that adequate measures and standards exist in order to comply with the conditions for the lawful processing of personal information; (c) a manual is developed, monitored, maintained and made available as prescribed in sections 14 and 51 of the Promotion of Access to Information Act, 2000; (d) internal measures are developed together with adequate systems to process requests for information or access thereto; and (e) internal awareness sessions are conducted regarding the provisions of the Act, regulations made in terms of the Act, codes of conduct, or information obtained from the Regulator.

sections.heading: A private or public body which is sufficiently representative of any class of
sections.summary: Private or public bodies can apply for codes of conduct to the Regulator on Form 3 for industry-specific standards.
sections.dtia_summary: Bodies can apply for codes of conduct via Form 3; Regulator may issue codes of conduct.

requirements.text: A private or public body which is sufficiently representative of any class of bodies, or of any industry, profession, or vocation that wishes to apply for the issuing of a code of conduct in terms of section 61(1)(b) of the Act, must submit an application to the Regulator on Form 3

sections.source_quote: A private or public body which is sufficiently representative of any class of bodies, or of any industry, profession, or vocation that wishes to apply for the issuing of a code of conduct in terms of section 61(1)(b) of the Act, must submit an application to the Regulator on Form 3.

sections.heading: A responsible party who wishes to process personal information of a data subject
sections.summary: Responsible parties must obtain written consent for direct marketing via electronic communication using Form 4.
sections.dtia_summary: Responsible parties must obtain written consent for direct marketing via Form 4.

requirements.text: A responsible party who wishes to process personal information of a data subject for the purpose of direct marketing by electronic communication must in terms of section 69(2) of the Act submit a request for written consent to that data subject on Form 4

sections.source_quote: A responsible party who wishes to process personal information of a data subject for the purpose of direct marketing by electronic communication must in terms of section 69(2) of the Act submit a request for written consent to that data subject on Form 4.

sections.summary: Complaints must be submitted to the Regulator on Form 5 for investigation under section 74 of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Complaints must be submitted to the Regulator on Form 5; Regulator handles complaints via Form 5.

requirements.text: Any person who wishes to submit a complaint contemplated in section 74(1) of the Act must submit such a complaint to the Regulator on Part I of Form 5
A responsible party or a data subject who wishes to submit a complaint contemplated in section 74(2) of the Act must submit such a complaint to the Regulator on Part II of Form 5

sections.source_quote: (1) Any person who wishes to submit a complaint contemplated in section 74(1) of the Act must submit such a complaint to the Regulator on Part I of Form 5. (2) A responsible party or a data subject who wishes to submit a complaint contemplated in section 74(2) of the Act must submit such a complaint to the Regulator on Part II of Form 5.

sections.summary: Regulator must inform data subjects and responsible parties of conciliation meetings and issues during complaint investigations.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulator must inform data subjects and responsible parties of conciliation meetings and issues during investigations.

requirements.text: If during the investigation of a complaint the Regulator decides to act as a conciliator in terms of section 76(1)(b) of the Act, and convene a conciliation meeting, the Regulator must, as soon as it is practically possible, inform the data subject and the responsible party implicated in the complaint on Form 6 of the following: (a) the Regulator's decision to act as a conciliator in the matter; and (b) the date, time and place of the conciliation meeting

The Regulator may consolidate separate complaints, which are alleged to relate to the same interference with the protection of personal information by the same responsible party, in order to deal with the complaints in the same conciliation proceedings
The Regulator must ensure that all persons entitled to attend the conciliation meeting are notified within a reasonable time, of the date, time and place of the meeting
The Regulator may request all the relevant documentation relating to the complaint from the data subject and the responsible party
The Regulator may confer with the parties in person, by electronic communication means, or by any other means as is deemed appropriate
Where a conciliation meeting fails to take place, the Regulator must arrange for an alternative date and notify the persons entitled to attend the conciliation meeting accordingly
The Regulator must issue a conciliation certificate on Form 7 within a reasonable time after the date of the conclusion of the conciliation meeting

If the complaint is not resolved, or either or both of the parties did not attend a conciliation meeting, the Regulator must proceed with the complaint as provided for in terms of section 76 of the Act

sections.source_quote: If during the investigation of a complaint the Regulator decides to act as a conciliator in terms of section 76(1)(b) of the Act, and convene a conciliation meeting, the Regulator must, as soon as it is practically possible, inform the data subject and the responsible party implicated in the complaint on Form 6 of the following: (a) the Regulator's decision to act as a conciliator in the matter; and (b) the date, time and place of the conciliation meeting. The Regulator- (a) may consolidate separate complaints, which are alleged to relate to the same interference with the protection of personal information by the same responsible party, in order to deal with the complaints in the same conciliation proceedings; (b) must ensure that all persons entitled to attend the conciliation meeting are notified within a reasonable time, of the date, time and place of the meeting; (c) may request all the relevant documentation relating to the complaint from the data subject and the responsible party; and (d) may confer with the parties in person, by electronic communication means, or by any other means as is deemed appropriate. Where a conciliation meeting fails to take place, the Regulator must arrange for an alternative date and notify the persons entitled to attend the conciliation meeting accordingly. The Regulator must issue a conciliation certificate on Form 7 within a reasonable time after the date of the conclusion of the conciliation meeting. If the complaint is not resolved, or either or both of the parties did not attend a conciliation meeting, the Regulator must proceed with the complaint as provided for in terms of section 76 of the Act.

sections.summary: Data subjects must submit a complaint form to the responsible party for data processing under Article 11(3)(a) of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Data subjects can request correction or deletion of personal information via Form 1; responsible parties must provide reasonable assistance at no cost.

requirements.text: Data subject must submit Form 1 to responsible party for data processing under Article 11(3)(a) of the Act
Responsible party must provide reasonable assistance at no cost for data subject to complete Form 1

sections.source_quote: „n Datasubjek wat beswaar wil aanteken teen die verwerking van persoonlike inligting ingevolge artikel 11(3)(a) van die Wet, moet die beswaar op Vorm 1 aan die verantwoordelike party voorlê. Die verantwoordelike party, of ‟n aangewese persoon, moet waar nodig redelike bystand lewer, kosteloos, om die datasubjek in staat te stel om ‟n beswaar op Vorm 1 te maak.

sections.summary: Data subjects must submit a request form to the responsible party for correction or deletion of personal information under Article 24(1) of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Data subjects can request correction or deletion of personal information via Form 2; responsible parties must provide reasonable assistance at no cost.

requirements.text: Data subject must submit Form 2 to responsible party for correction or deletion of personal information under Article 24(1) of the Act
Responsible party must provide reasonable assistance at no cost for data subject to complete Form 2

sections.source_quote: „n Datasubjek wat ‟n regstelling of skrapping van persoonlike inligting of die vernietiging of skrapping van ‟n rekord van persoonlike inligting ingevolge artikel 24(1) van die Wet wil aansoek doen, moet ‟n versoek op Vorm 2 aan die verantwoordelike party voorlê. Die verantwoordelike party, of ‟n aangewese persoon, moet waar nodig redelike bystand lewer, gratis, sodat ‟n datasubjek Vorm 2 kan invul.

sections.summary: Information officers must develop, implement, monitor and maintain a compliance framework.
sections.dtia_summary: Information officers must establish a compliance framework, conduct impact assessments, develop guidelines, and hold awareness sessions.

requirements.text: Information officer must develop, implement, monitor and maintain a compliance framework
Information officer must conduct personal information impact assessments
Information officer must develop, monitor, maintain and provide guidelines as per the Access to Information Act
Information officer must develop internal policies and systems for information requests
Information officer must hold internal awareness sessions on the Act, regulations, codes of conduct, and regulatory information

sections.source_quote: „n Inligtingsbeampte moet, bykomend tot die verantwoordelikhede in artikel 55(1) van die Wet beoog, verseker dat- (a) ‟n voldoeningsraamwerk ontwikkel, geïmplementeer, gemonitor en onderhou word; (b) ‟n persoonlike inligtingsimpakassessering gedoen word om te verseker dat voldoende maatreëls en standaarde bestaan ten einde te voldoen aan die voorwaardes vir die wettige verwerking van persoonlike inligting; (c) ‟n handleiding ontwikkel, gemonitor, onderhou en beskikbaar gestel word soos in artikels 14 en 51 van die Wet op Bevordering van Toegang tot Inligting, 2000 (Wet van 2000), voorgeskryf; (d) interne maatreëls ontwikkel word saam met voldoende stelsels om versoeke om inligting of toegang daartoe te verwerk; en (e) interne bewustheidsessies oor die bepalings van die Wet, regulasies ingevolge die Wet uitgevaardig, gedragskode, of inligting van die Reguleerder verkry, gehou word.

sections.heading: ‟n Privaat of openbare liggaam wat voldoende verteenwoordigend van enige klas
sections.summary: Organizations must submit a request form to the regulator for the issuance of a code of conduct under Article 61(1)(b) of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Organizations can request a code of conduct via Form 3; regulator must process the request.

requirements.text: Organization must submit Form 3 to regulator for code of conduct under Article 61(1)(b) of the Act

sections.source_quote: „n Privaat of openbare liggaam wat voldoende verteenwoordigend van enige klas van liggame, of van enige nywerheid, professie of ambag is wat om die uitreiking van ‟n gedragskode ingevolge artikel 61(1)(b) van die Wet wil aansoek doen, moet ‟n aansoek op Vorm 3 aan die Reguleerder voorlê.

sections.heading: „n Verantwoordelike party wat die persoonlike inligting van ‟n datasubjek wil
sections.summary: Responsible parties must obtain written consent from data subjects for direct marketing via electronic communication under Article 69(2) of the Act.

requirements.text: Responsible party must obtain written consent from data subject for direct marketing via electronic communication under Article 69(2) of the Act

sections.source_quote: „n Verantwoordelike party wat die persoonlike inligting van ‟n datasubjek wil verwerk met die oog op regstreekse bemarking deur elektroniese kommunikasie moet ingevolge artikel 69(2) van die Wet ‟n versoek om skriftelike toestemming op Vorm 4 aan daardie datasubjek voorlê.

sections.summary: Complaints must be submitted to the regulator in the form of Form 5.

requirements.text: Complainant must submit complaint to regulator on Form 5

sections.source_quote: Enigiemand wat ‟n klagte in artikel 74(1) van die Wet bedoel, wil voorlê, moet sodanige klagte op Deel I van Vorm 5 aan die Reguleerder voorlê.

sections.summary: Regulator may act as mediator during complaint investigations.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulator may act as mediator during complaint investigations; must inform parties of mediation proceedings.

sections.source_quote: Indien die Reguleerder tydens die ondersoek van ‟n klagte besluit om ingevolge artikel 76(1)(b) van die Wet as ‟n konsiliator op te tree, en ‟n konsiliasievergadering saamroep, moet die Reguleerder, so gou as prakties moontlik, die datasubjek en die geïmpliseerde verantwoordelike party op Vorm 6 oor die volgende in kennis stel: (a) die Reguleerder se besluit om as ‟n konsiliator in die aangeleentheid op te tree; en (b) die datum, tyd en plek van die konsiliasievergadering.

sections.summary: Regulator must notify parties before conducting investigations.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulator must notify parties before conducting investigations; parties must respond to notifications.

requirements.text: Regulator must notify parties before conducting investigations
Regulator must inform responsible party of complaint or investigation subject

sections.source_quote: Indien die Reguleerder voornemens is om enige aangeleentheid beoog in Hoofstuk 10 van die Wet beoog, te ondersoek, moet die Reguleerder ingevolge artikel 79 van die Wet die partye op wie die ondersoek betrekking het, op Deel I van Vorm 8 verwittig van sodanige voorneme voordat die ondersoek gedoen word.

sections.summary: Regulator may facilitate settlements between parties during complaint investigations.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulator may facilitate settlements between parties; must issue settlement certificates.

requirements.text: Regulator may facilitate settlements between parties
Regulator must issue settlement certificate on Form 10

sections.source_quote: Indien dit uit ‟n klagte of enige skriftelike antwoord op die klagte kragtens artikel 79(b)(ii) van die Wet of tydens ‟n konsiliasievergadering, voorkom dat dit moontlik kan wees om ‟n skikking tussen die partye en indien gepas, bevredigende versekerings soos beoog in artikel 80 van die Wet, te verkry, kan die Reguleerder persoonlik oorleg pleeg met die partye, per elektroniese kommunikasiemiddele, of per enige ander middele soos gepas geag word om te poog om ‟n skikking te verkry en indien van toepassing, bevredigende versekerings soos beoog in artikel 80 van die Wet.

sections.summary: Regulator must conduct audits and notify parties of audit findings.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulator must conduct audits; must notify parties of audit findings.

requirements.text: Regulator must conduct audits
Regulator must notify parties of audit findings

sections.source_quote: „n Versoek om ‟n oudit ingevolge artikel 89(1) van die Wet moet op Deel 1 van Vorm 11 aan die Reguleerder voorgelê word.

sections.summary: Regulator must inform parties of developments during investigations.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulator must inform parties of developments during investigations; must notify parties of investigation outcomes.

requirements.text: Regulator must inform parties of developments during investigations
Regulator must notify parties of investigation outcomes

sections.source_quote: Deur die loop van ‟n ondersoek moet die Reguleerder binne ‟n redelike tyd vanaf die datum waarop ‟n besluit geneem of stap gedoen word- (a) die klaer, die datasubjek (indien nie die klaer nie) en die verantwoordelike party ingelig hou van die ontwikkelings in die ondersoek; en (b) die klaer, datasubjek (indien nie klaer nie) en die verantwoordelike party verwittig van die uitslag van die ondersoek.

sections.heading: Hierdie Regulasies heet die Regulasies op die Beskerming van Persoonlike Inligting

sections.source_quote: „n Datasubjek wat ‟n regstelling of skrapping van persoonlike inligting of die vernietiging of skrapping van ‟n rekord van persoonlike inligting ingevolge artikel 24(1) van die Wet wil aansoek doen, moet ‟n versoek op Vorm 2 aan die verantwoordelike party voorlê. Die verantwoordelike party, of ‟n aangewese persoon, moet waar nodig redelike bystand lewer, gratis, sodat ‟n datasubjek Vorm 2 kan invul.
